event: admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU)
no resolution of mitral valve vegetation
pansystolic murmur over mitral valve area
unable to move ankle due to pain
unable to move ankle due to weakness
elevated white blood cell count 13 x 109/L
transoesophageal echocardiogram showed posterior mitral valve leaflet vegetation
prolonged antibiotic therapy with no improvement
exposure of distal superficial femoral artery (SFA)
vascular reconstruction with reversed saphenous vein graft
end-to-side proximal anastomosis to distal SFA
vein graft tunnelled anatomically to midcalf
anastomosed end-to-side to posterior tibial artery
no microorganisms recovered from mitral vegetations
no microorganisms recovered from aneurysm thrombus
discharged from ICU on day seven
echocardiography showed normal mitral valve function at five months
palpable right posterior tibial artery pulse at ankle
advised to remain on aspirin 75 mg daily
